chapter: Chapter 2: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye

questions.question: Why are microscopes needed to study microorganisms?
questions.answer: Microorganisms are extremely small and cannot be seen with the naked eye, so microscopes are required to observe them.

questions.question: What did Robert Hooke observe when he examined cork under a microscope?
questions.answer: He observed tiny compartments which he named cells.

questions.question: Why is Antonie van Leeuwenhoek considered an important scientist in microbiology?
questions.answer: He was the first to observe living microorganisms using improved microscopes.

questions.question: Name the three main parts of a cell.
questions.answer: Cell membrane, cytoplasm, and nucleus.

questions.question: What role does the nucleus play in a cell?
questions.answer: The nucleus controls the activities and functions of the cell.

questions.question: What is the function of cytoplasm in the cell?
questions.answer: Cytoplasm contains organelles and is the site for most metabolic processes.

questions.question: How do plant cells differ from animal cells?
questions.answer: Plant cells have a cell wall and chloroplasts, while animal cells do not.

questions.question: Why do cells vary in shape and size?
questions.answer: Cells vary because they perform different specialized functions.

questions.question: What are tissues?
questions.answer: Tissues are groups of similar cells performing the same function.

questions.question: How are organs formed in living organisms?
questions.answer: Organs are formed by groups of tissues working together.

questions.question: Give two examples of microorganisms.
questions.answer: Bacteria and protozoa.

questions.question: How do microorganisms help in decomposition?
questions.answer: They break down dead plants and animals into simpler substances.

questions.question: What is compost?
questions.answer: Compost is nutrient-rich manure formed by decomposition of organic waste.

questions.question: What role does yeast play in bread making?
questions.answer: Yeast ferments sugar and produces carbon dioxide that makes dough rise.

questions.question: How does Lactobacillus convert milk into curd?
questions.answer: It converts lactose in milk into lactic acid.

questions.question: What is nitrogen fixation?
questions.answer: It is the conversion of atmospheric nitrogen into usable forms for plants.

questions.question: Which bacteria help plants obtain nitrogen from the soil?
questions.answer: Rhizobium bacteria.

questions.question: Why are microorganisms important in agriculture?
questions.answer: They improve soil fertility and recycle nutrients.

questions.question: What are unicellular organisms?
questions.answer: Organisms made up of a single cell.

questions.question: Give one example of a unicellular organism.
questions.answer: Amoeba.

questions.question: What is the main function of decomposer microorganisms?
questions.answer: They recycle nutrients by breaking down organic matter.

questions.question: Why are microalgae important in aquatic ecosystems?
questions.answer: They produce oxygen and serve as food for aquatic organisms.

questions.question: What is fermentation?
questions.answer: Fermentation is the process in which microorganisms convert sugars into gases or acids.

questions.question: Why are cells called the building blocks of life?
questions.answer: Because all living organisms are made up of cells.

questions.question: How do microorganisms maintain ecological balance?
questions.answer: They recycle nutrients and support food chains in ecosystems.
